title: spiraea betulifolia var. subcordata
intro: Spiraea betulifolia var. subcordata is a variety of the Betula-leaved Spiraea, a type of flowering shrub native to Korea and parts of China, characterized by its compact growth and beautiful white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The health benefits of Spiraea betulifolia var. subcordata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
